seo.title: LUNA Programs | Assessment Preparation & Academic Support
seo.description: Explore LUNA Studies programs including MAP, WIDA, CAT4, AEIS, TOEFL, IELTS, JLPT, HSK, English, Math, Japanese and Mandarin support.

hero.label: Luna Programs
hero.description: From assessment preparation to long-term academic support, each Luna program is designed with clear goals, personalised guidance and measurable progress.

assessment.label: Exam & Assessment Preparation
assessment.title: Assessment Preparation
assessment.description: Targeted support for international school assessments, entrance exams, and language proficiency tests.

academic.label: Academic & Language Support
academic.title: Academic Support
academic.description: Long-term subject support for English, math, Japanese, Mandarin, speaking, writing, and school learning.

map.title: MAP Preparation
map.body: NWEA MAP Growth preparation for Reading, Language Usage and Mathematics with RIT-score awareness.
map.details: NWEA MAP Growth is an adaptive assessment: the question difficulty changes as the student answers, and results are reported through RIT scores that track academic growth over time. Luna MAP Preparation helps students understand the style of MAP Reading, Language Usage and Mathematics without treating the test as simple memorisation. Lessons focus on reading stamina, academic vocabulary, grammar accuracy, multi-step math reasoning and calm decision-making when questions become harder. We also help families interpret practice patterns so students know whether they need comprehension work, language usage review, number sense, geometry, data analysis or word-problem strategy before the next school assessment.
map.suitableFor: Students in international schools using NWEA MAP Growth, students applying to schools that request MAP results, and families who want clearer RIT-score growth targets in Reading, Language Usage or Mathematics.

detailPage.heroLine: Build readiness for MAP Growth adaptive testing through RIT-aware practice in reading, language usage and mathematics.
detailPage.overviewTitle: Adaptive MAP preparation built around RIT growth, not random drills.
detailPage.overview: Luna prepares students for the way NWEA MAP Growth actually works: adaptive questions, changing difficulty, and RIT scores that show growth across terms. We identify whether the student is losing points through vocabulary gaps, rushed reading, grammar uncertainty, weak number sense or unfamiliar math wording. Then lessons target those gaps with MAP-style practice, explanation, review and progress tracking. The goal is not to memorise answers, but to help students become stronger readers, clearer language users and more flexible mathematical thinkers.
detailPage.coverTitle: What we cover in MAP Reading, Language Usage and Mathematics.
cover: MAP Reading comprehension: main idea, inference, author purpose, vocabulary in context and evidence-based answers
MAP Language Usage: grammar, punctuation, sentence structure, usage conventions and revision questions
MAP Mathematics: number operations, algebraic thinking, geometry, measurement, data and multi-step word problems
RIT-score goal setting so students understand which skill bands need the most attention
Adaptive test habits: pacing, checking, eliminating choices and staying calm as questions become harder
Practice review that separates careless mistakes from true concept gaps
detailPage.approachTitle: A RIT-aware plan for measurable academic growth.
detailPage.approachDescription: We begin by reviewing the student’s current level, school expectations and recent MAP or practice performance. Lessons then combine targeted skill instruction, MAP-style questions and short reflection after mistakes. Parents receive clearer feedback on which areas are improving and which skills still need reinforcement before the next testing window.
lunaHelps: Map weaker skill areas before lessons begin, including reading, grammar, vocabulary and math reasoning
Connect practice to RIT-style growth targets instead of assigning generic worksheets
Teach students how to handle adaptive difficulty without panic or guessing too quickly
Build academic vocabulary that appears across MAP Reading, Language Usage and Mathematics
Track progress so families can see whether accuracy, pacing and confidence are improving
detailPage.outcomesTitle: MAP outcomes students can build over time.
outcomes: Stronger comprehension and vocabulary for MAP Reading passages
More accurate grammar and sentence decisions in Language Usage
Improved math reasoning for word problems and unfamiliar question formats
Better pacing and confidence during adaptive assessments
Clearer understanding of learning targets connected to RIT-score growth
detailPage.tutorTitle: Matched with tutors who understand international school assessments.
detailPage.tutorDescription: Luna matches MAP students with tutors who can explain reading, language usage or math skills in a way that fits the student’s grade level and school background. The tutor helps the student practise strategically, review mistakes carefully and build the academic habits needed for long-term MAP Growth improvement.

faq.q: What is NWEA MAP Growth?
faq.a: MAP Growth is an adaptive assessment used by many international schools to measure academic progress. It adjusts question difficulty and reports results with RIT scores.

faq.q: Can students prepare for an adaptive test?
faq.a: Yes. Students cannot memorise the test, but they can strengthen the reading, language and math skills that MAP measures and learn better pacing and review habits.

faq.q: Do you help with RIT scores?
faq.a: We help families understand what RIT-score patterns suggest and use them to prioritise skill areas for lessons.

faq.q: Which MAP subjects can Luna support?
faq.a: We support MAP Reading, Language Usage and Mathematics, depending on the student’s grade level and needs.

seo.title: NWEA MAP Growth Preparation | RIT Score Reading & Math Support | Luna Studies
seo.description: Personalised NWEA MAP Growth preparation for Reading, Language Usage and Mathematics with RIT-score awareness, adaptive test strategies and international school support.

wida.title: WIDA Preparation
wida.body: English language proficiency support across listening, speaking, reading and writing for WIDA-style tasks.
wida.details: WIDA assessments look at how well multilingual learners use English for school, not just whether they know grammar rules. Students need to listen to classroom language, explain ideas aloud, read academic texts and write organised responses using appropriate vocabulary. Luna WIDA Preparation builds these four domains together. Lessons include speaking frames, listening tasks, reading comprehension, sentence expansion, paragraph development and academic word practice. We help students move from short answers to clearer explanations so they can participate more confidently in international school classrooms and WIDA-style assessments.
wida.suitableFor: English language learners in international or bilingual schools who need stronger listening, speaking, reading and writing skills for WIDA ACCESS, WIDA Screener or classroom academic English.

detailPage.heroLine: Develop school-ready English across WIDA listening, speaking, reading and writing domains.
detailPage.overviewTitle: WIDA preparation for real academic language use.
detailPage.overview: WIDA is different from a simple English test because it measures how students use English to learn, explain, compare, describe and justify ideas in school. Luna helps students practise the four language domains in connected ways: listening for key information, speaking in complete responses, reading grade-level academic language and writing with structure. Lessons are especially helpful for students who understand content but struggle to express ideas clearly in English. We build confidence gradually with sentence frames, vocabulary banks, guided speaking and writing feedback.
detailPage.coverTitle: What we cover across the four WIDA language domains.
cover: Listening for classroom instructions, academic details, sequence, cause and effect, and speaker purpose
Speaking practice using complete sentences, explanations, comparisons and evidence-based answers
Reading support for nonfiction texts, diagrams, academic vocabulary and comprehension questions
Writing development from sentence expansion to organised paragraphs and short constructed responses
Academic vocabulary for science, social studies, math explanations and classroom discussion
Confidence routines for students who hesitate to speak or write in English
detailPage.approachTitle: Language growth through guided practice, not isolated grammar drills.
detailPage.approachDescription: We identify which WIDA domains feel hardest for the student and then connect them through meaningful tasks. A lesson may begin with listening, move into speaking, then finish with a written response. This helps students use vocabulary actively and transfer English skills into classroom learning.
lunaHelps: Build stronger spoken explanations for WIDA-style speaking prompts
Improve listening accuracy for classroom and assessment language
Teach students how to organise written answers with clearer sentence control
Expand academic vocabulary for school subjects, not only everyday conversation
Support confidence so students participate more actively in English-medium classes
detailPage.outcomesTitle: English proficiency outcomes for school success.
outcomes: More confident speaking in complete academic responses
Better listening comprehension for instructions and content details
Stronger reading of informational and school-based texts
More organised writing with clearer grammar and vocabulary
Improved readiness for WIDA-style English proficiency tasks
detailPage.tutorTitle: Matched with tutors who understand multilingual learners.
detailPage.tutorDescription: WIDA learners need patient language support and clear feedback. Luna matches students with tutors who can scaffold English without making lessons feel childish, helping them build school vocabulary, speaking confidence and writing accuracy at the right pace.

faq.q: What does WIDA measure?
faq.a: WIDA measures English language proficiency across listening, speaking, reading and writing in academic school contexts.

faq.q: Is WIDA preparation only grammar practice?
faq.a: No. Grammar matters, but WIDA also requires students to understand texts, explain ideas orally and write organised academic responses.

faq.q: Can Luna help shy speakers?
faq.a: Yes. We use sentence frames, guided prompts and repeated practice to help students speak with more confidence.

faq.q: Do lessons support school English too?
faq.a: Yes. WIDA skills are closely connected to classroom participation, reading, writing and subject vocabulary.

seo.title: WIDA Preparation for English Language Proficiency | Luna Studies
seo.description: WIDA preparation for multilingual learners covering listening, speaking, reading and writing with academic vocabulary and school English support.

cat4.title: CAT4 Preparation
cat4.body: Reasoning preparation for verbal, quantitative, non-verbal and spatial CAT4 batteries.
cat4.details: CAT4 assesses how students reason with words, numbers, shapes and spatial patterns. It is not a memorisation test, so preparation should focus on thinking habits rather than drilling facts. Luna CAT4 Preparation introduces the four major reasoning areas: verbal reasoning, quantitative reasoning, non-verbal reasoning and spatial reasoning. Students learn to compare, classify, rotate, sequence, infer and eliminate options. We help them slow down when patterns look unfamiliar, explain their logic and build flexible problem-solving strategies for school placement or ability testing.
cat4.suitableFor: Students preparing for CAT4 assessments, international school placement, ability screening or reasoning-based admissions tasks.

detailPage.heroLine: Strengthen CAT4 reasoning across verbal, quantitative, non-verbal and spatial tasks.
detailPage.overviewTitle: CAT4 preparation that builds reasoning, not memorised answers.
detailPage.overview: CAT4 questions can feel strange because they test how students think rather than what they recently studied. Luna helps students become familiar with the logic behind each battery: verbal relationships, number patterns, figure classification, matrices, rotations and spatial visualisation. Instead of giving students shortcuts only, we teach them how to observe details, test rules, compare options and explain why an answer fits. This creates calmer problem-solving habits when students meet abstract or unfamiliar questions.
detailPage.coverTitle: What we cover in the four CAT4 reasoning batteries.
cover: Verbal reasoning: word relationships, analogies, categories and logical language patterns
Quantitative reasoning: number analogies, series, equations, relationships and numerical logic
Non-verbal reasoning: figure classification, matrices, pattern recognition and visual rules
Spatial reasoning: shape rotation, folding, transformation and mental visualisation
Elimination strategies for confusing answer choices and time pressure
Reflection routines so students can explain the rule behind each solution
detailPage.approachTitle: Reasoning skills developed through pattern awareness and explanation.
detailPage.approachDescription: We introduce CAT4 question types step by step and teach students to verbalise the rule they see. This reduces guessing and helps tutors identify whether the student is struggling with vocabulary, number logic, visual discrimination, spatial awareness or test pacing.
lunaHelps: Demystify unfamiliar CAT4 formats before the student faces them in testing
Strengthen pattern recognition across verbal, number and visual tasks
Teach elimination and checking strategies for abstract reasoning questions
Build confidence for students who panic when a question has no obvious school formula
Develop flexible thinking habits useful beyond CAT4
detailPage.outcomesTitle: Reasoning outcomes students can transfer to school learning.
outcomes: Stronger verbal and quantitative reasoning confidence
Improved recognition of visual and spatial patterns
Better ability to explain logic rather than guess
More calmness with unfamiliar test formats
Sharper problem-solving habits for placement assessments
detailPage.tutorTitle: Matched with tutors who teach reasoning clearly.
detailPage.tutorDescription: CAT4 support works best when the tutor can make invisible thinking visible. Luna tutors guide students through the reasoning process, ask them to explain patterns and help them build strategies for each battery without pretending the test is simple memorisation.

faq.q: Can CAT4 be prepared for?
faq.a: Students cannot memorise CAT4 content, but they can learn question formats, reasoning strategies and calmer ways to approach unfamiliar tasks.

faq.q: What are the four CAT4 areas?
faq.a: CAT4 commonly assesses verbal, quantitative, non-verbal and spatial reasoning.

faq.q: Is CAT4 the same as a school exam?
faq.a: No. It focuses more on reasoning and cognitive ability than curriculum knowledge.

faq.q: Who benefits from CAT4 preparation?
faq.a: Students applying to schools, preparing for ability testing or needing stronger abstract reasoning habits can benefit.

seo.title: CAT4 Preparation | Verbal, Quantitative, Non-Verbal & Spatial Reasoning | Luna Studies
seo.description: CAT4 preparation focused on verbal reasoning, quantitative reasoning, non-verbal patterns and spatial reasoning for school placement and ability testing.

aeis.title: AEIS Preparation
aeis.body: Singapore school entry preparation for AEIS English and Mathematics.
aeis.details: AEIS is a demanding entrance pathway for international students seeking admission to Singapore government schools. Students need English strong enough to understand passages, grammar, vocabulary and writing tasks, plus Mathematics accuracy across grade-level concepts and word problems. Luna AEIS Preparation builds both subjects with a structured plan. We focus on reading comprehension, composition planning, grammar control, problem-solving methods and exam endurance. Lessons also help families understand the student’s current readiness and the gap between present level and Singapore school entry expectations.
aeis.suitableFor: International students preparing for AEIS or Singapore school entry who need focused support in English, Mathematics or both subjects.

detailPage.heroLine: Prepare for AEIS English and Mathematics with a structured Singapore school entry plan.
detailPage.overviewTitle: AEIS preparation for the English and Math demands of Singapore school entry.
detailPage.overview: AEIS requires more than general English or routine math practice. Students must read accurately, write clearly, understand grammar, solve word problems and work under exam pressure. Luna helps students build the two core pillars of AEIS preparation: English language control and mathematical problem-solving. We diagnose whether the biggest barrier is vocabulary, composition structure, grammar accuracy, calculation fluency, concept gaps or multi-step reasoning. Lessons then combine instruction, practice and feedback so preparation is steady instead of last-minute cramming.
detailPage.coverTitle: What we cover for AEIS English and Mathematics.
cover: AEIS English reading comprehension, vocabulary in context and inference questions
Grammar accuracy, sentence correction, cloze-style practice and usage review
Composition planning, paragraph development, narrative clarity and written expression
Mathematics concepts aligned to the student’s target level and school entry pathway
Word-problem strategies, model drawing, multi-step reasoning and checking routines
Timed practice, homework feedback and readiness review before the exam period
detailPage.approachTitle: A two-subject plan for Singapore entry readiness.
detailPage.approachDescription: We build English and Mathematics together because AEIS success often depends on both. Students receive targeted lessons, structured homework and review of recurring mistakes. The plan changes as the student’s accuracy, writing control and problem-solving confidence improve.
lunaHelps: Assess the student’s English and Mathematics readiness for AEIS demands
Build vocabulary and writing structure needed for English tasks
Strengthen mathematical concepts before moving into harder exam-style problems
Teach students to decode word problems instead of guessing operations
Provide a steady preparation rhythm with feedback after practice
detailPage.outcomesTitle: AEIS outcomes linked to school entry preparation.
outcomes: Stronger English reading comprehension and grammar control
More organised composition planning and writing fluency
Improved math accuracy across target-level concepts
Better word-problem reasoning under exam conditions
Clearer readiness for Singapore school entry expectations
detailPage.tutorTitle: Matched with tutors for AEIS English, Math or both.
detailPage.tutorDescription: Luna matches AEIS students with tutors who can support the correct subject combination and target level. Tutors help students understand Singapore-style expectations, practise consistently and close gaps that affect entrance readiness.

faq.q: Does AEIS include both English and Math?
faq.a: Yes. AEIS preparation usually requires strong English and Mathematics, though the exact focus depends on the student’s target level.

faq.q: Why is AEIS challenging for international students?
faq.a: Students must handle academic English while also solving grade-level math problems, often under time pressure.

faq.q: Can Luna help with composition writing?
faq.a: Yes. We work on planning, paragraph flow, grammar accuracy, vocabulary and clearer expression.

faq.q: How early should students prepare?
faq.a: Earlier preparation is usually better because both English foundations and math problem-solving take time to build.

seo.title: AEIS Preparation for Singapore School Entry | English & Mathematics | Luna Studies
seo.description: Structured AEIS preparation for international students applying to Singapore government schools, covering English, composition, Mathematics and exam strategies.

toefl.title: TOEFL Preparation
toefl.body: Academic English preparation for TOEFL iBT, university pathways and school admissions.
toefl.details: TOEFL measures whether students can use English in academic settings: reading university-style texts, listening to lectures, speaking with structure and writing evidence-based responses. Luna TOEFL Preparation focuses on the academic English behind the score. Students build reading speed, note-taking, listening accuracy, speaking organisation, integrated writing and independent essay skills. We also review vocabulary, grammar and response structure so students can communicate ideas clearly under time limits. This program is especially useful for learners preparing for international school, university or English proficiency requirements.
toefl.suitableFor: Students preparing for TOEFL iBT, TOEFL Junior, university applications, international school admissions or academic English proficiency requirements.

detailPage.heroLine: Prepare for TOEFL with academic reading, listening, speaking and writing strategies.
detailPage.overviewTitle: TOEFL preparation for academic English performance.
detailPage.overview: TOEFL rewards students who can understand academic content and respond clearly, not just students who know grammar rules. Luna lessons train the skills behind each section: reading dense passages, listening for lecture structure, taking useful notes, building spoken responses and writing organised essays. We help students identify whether their score is limited by vocabulary, timing, listening detail, pronunciation, grammar or weak response organisation. The preparation is practical and structured, with regular feedback so students know exactly what to improve next.
detailPage.coverTitle: What we cover across TOEFL sections.
cover: TOEFL Reading: passage mapping, inference, vocabulary, sentence simplification and timing
TOEFL Listening: lecture structure, detail recognition, speaker attitude and note-taking
TOEFL Speaking: response templates, fluency, pronunciation, examples and time control
TOEFL Writing: integrated writing, independent essays, coherence, grammar and academic vocabulary
Academic word families, paraphrasing and sentence variety for stronger expression
Mock task review with section-specific feedback and score-growth priorities
detailPage.approachTitle: Academic English first, test strategy second.
detailPage.approachDescription: We combine TOEFL question practice with real skill building. Students learn how to understand academic material, organise notes and express ideas in a concise way. Each mistake review points to a specific improvement: vocabulary, structure, timing, accuracy or clarity.
lunaHelps: Diagnose which TOEFL sections are holding the student back most
Teach note-taking systems for lectures and integrated speaking or writing tasks
Build academic vocabulary and reading stamina for longer passages
Improve spoken response structure under strict time limits
Provide detailed writing feedback on logic, grammar, cohesion and task fulfilment
detailPage.outcomesTitle: TOEFL outcomes for academic and university readiness.
outcomes: Faster and more accurate academic reading
Better listening notes and lecture comprehension
Clearer spoken responses with stronger organisation
More coherent integrated and independent writing
Greater confidence for TOEFL and academic English environments
detailPage.tutorTitle: Matched with tutors who understand academic English testing.
detailPage.tutorDescription: TOEFL students need feedback that is specific and practical. Luna tutors help learners improve section by section while also strengthening the academic English needed for school, university and international study.

faq.q: Does Luna support TOEFL iBT?
faq.a: Yes. Lessons can cover TOEFL iBT Reading, Listening, Speaking and Writing.

faq.q: Can you help with TOEFL Speaking?
faq.a: Yes. We practise timed responses, structure, examples, fluency and pronunciation clarity.

faq.q: Do students receive writing feedback?
faq.a: Yes. We review essays for task response, organisation, grammar, vocabulary and coherence.

faq.q: Is TOEFL preparation useful for university readiness?
faq.a: Yes. TOEFL skills overlap strongly with academic reading, lecture listening, note-taking and writing.

seo.title: TOEFL Preparation for Academic English & University Applications | Luna Studies
seo.description: TOEFL iBT preparation for reading, listening, speaking and writing with academic English strategies, essay feedback and university readiness support.

ielts.title: IELTS Preparation
ielts.body: IELTS Academic and General Training support for band-score improvement and exam strategy.
ielts.details: IELTS preparation should connect language improvement with band-score criteria. Luna IELTS Preparation helps learners understand what examiners look for in speaking and writing, while building accuracy and speed in reading and listening. Lessons focus on Task 1 and Task 2 writing, speaking fluency, pronunciation, lexical resource, grammar range, reading timing and listening accuracy. We help students move from vague practice to targeted band improvement by reviewing performance against clear criteria and giving practical strategies for the next attempt.
ielts.suitableFor: Learners preparing for IELTS Academic or General Training who need band-score improvement for school, university, migration or professional goals.

detailPage.heroLine: Improve IELTS band readiness through targeted writing, speaking, reading and listening preparation.
detailPage.overviewTitle: IELTS preparation guided by band descriptors and exam strategy.
detailPage.overview: IELTS scores improve when students understand both the English skill and the scoring criteria. Luna helps learners prepare with a band-score mindset: what makes a Task 2 essay coherent, how Task 1 should report data, why speaking answers need development, and how reading and listening timing affect accuracy. Lessons are personalised around the student’s target band and weakest sections. Feedback is specific, so students know whether to work on task response, cohesion, vocabulary, grammar range, pronunciation, detail listening or skimming strategy.
detailPage.coverTitle: What we cover for IELTS Academic and General Training.
cover: IELTS Writing Task 1: data reports, letters, overview statements, comparisons and structure
IELTS Writing Task 2: argument essays, planning, cohesion, examples and grammar accuracy
IELTS Speaking: Part 1, Part 2 and Part 3 fluency, pronunciation and idea development
IELTS Reading: skimming, scanning, question types, timing and trap avoidance
IELTS Listening: prediction, signposting, detail accuracy and spelling control
Band descriptor feedback for writing and speaking improvement
detailPage.approachTitle: Targeted practice connected to the student’s target band.
detailPage.approachDescription: We start from the learner’s current level and target band. Lessons combine skill instruction, timed tasks, model analysis and feedback based on IELTS criteria. Students learn how to produce stronger answers, not just complete more papers.
lunaHelps: Identify which IELTS sections limit the student’s current band score
Teach writing structures that match Task 1 and Task 2 expectations
Improve speaking answers through guided development and pronunciation feedback
Build reading and listening strategies for time pressure and question traps
Give practical band-focused feedback after essays and mock speaking tasks
detailPage.outcomesTitle: IELTS outcomes for clearer band-score progress.
outcomes: More organised essays with stronger task response and cohesion
Clearer speaking answers with better fluency and development
Improved reading timing and question-type awareness
Better listening accuracy, spelling and detail recognition
A clearer plan for moving toward the student’s target band
detailPage.tutorTitle: Matched with tutors who give band-focused feedback.
detailPage.tutorDescription: IELTS learners need more than encouragement. Luna tutors explain how answers are judged, show examples of stronger responses and help students practise in a way that connects directly to band-score improvement.

faq.q: Do you support IELTS Academic and General Training?
faq.a: Yes. We can support both versions, with writing tasks adjusted to the student’s goal.

faq.q: Can Luna help raise my writing band?
faq.a: Yes. We focus on task response, structure, cohesion, vocabulary, grammar range and recurring errors.

faq.q: Do you provide speaking practice?
faq.a: Yes. Lessons can include mock speaking, answer expansion, pronunciation and confidence practice.

faq.q: How is IELTS different from general English?
faq.a: IELTS requires specific task awareness, timing and band-descriptor performance in addition to strong English skills.

seo.title: IELTS Preparation for Band Score Improvement | Academic & General | Luna Studies
seo.description: IELTS Academic and General preparation focused on band-score improvement, Writing Task 1 and 2, Speaking practice, Reading and Listening strategy.

jlpt.title: JLPT Preparation
jlpt.body: Japanese proficiency preparation from JLPT N5 to N1 with vocabulary, grammar, reading and listening support.
jlpt.details: JLPT preparation changes significantly from N5 to N1. Beginner levels require hiragana, katakana, basic kanji and essential sentence patterns, while higher levels demand faster reading, abstract vocabulary, complex grammar and stronger listening stamina. Luna JLPT Preparation builds a level-specific plan for vocabulary, kanji, grammar, reading and listening. Students practise exam-style questions but also learn how Japanese structures work so they can retain language more effectively. Lessons can support complete beginners, school learners and advanced students aiming for N3, N2 or N1 certification.
jlpt.suitableFor: Students preparing for JLPT N5, N4, N3, N2 or N1, as well as learners who want structured Japanese proficiency development before taking the exam.

detailPage.heroLine: Prepare for JLPT N5 to N1 with level-specific vocabulary, grammar, reading and listening practice.
detailPage.overviewTitle: JLPT preparation matched to the learner’s target level.
detailPage.overview: JLPT success depends on studying the right material for the right level. Luna helps learners avoid scattered study by creating a progression for vocabulary, kanji, grammar, reading and listening. At N5 and N4, lessons focus on foundations and sentence patterns. At N3, students learn to manage longer texts and more natural listening. At N2 and N1, we work on advanced vocabulary, nuance, speed and accuracy. Students also review mock questions carefully so they understand why an answer is correct, not just what to memorise.
detailPage.coverTitle: What we cover from JLPT N5 to N1.
cover: Level-specific vocabulary, kanji and expression building for N5, N4, N3, N2 or N1
Grammar patterns with clear explanations, example sentences and contrast practice
Reading comprehension for short notices, passages, essays and abstract texts by level
Listening practice for conversations, announcements, natural speed and key detail recognition
Mock question review with mistake tracking and exam-day pacing habits
Study routines for retention, spaced review and vocabulary recycling
detailPage.approachTitle: A structured path through Japanese levels, not a random word list.
detailPage.approachDescription: We begin with the learner’s current Japanese level and target JLPT date. Tutors then organise lessons around the vocabulary, grammar and skills needed for that level. Practice is reinforced through review, examples and exam-style tasks so students can use Japanese more confidently beyond the test.
lunaHelps: Choose a realistic JLPT target level based on current Japanese ability
Explain grammar patterns clearly with usage differences and examples
Build kanji and vocabulary through repeated, organised review
Improve reading speed for level-appropriate texts and question types
Develop listening stamina for natural Japanese conversations and announcements
detailPage.outcomesTitle: JLPT outcomes for Japanese proficiency growth.
outcomes: Stronger vocabulary and kanji retention for the target level
Clearer understanding of Japanese grammar patterns and nuance
Improved reading speed and comprehension accuracy
Better listening confidence with exam-style audio
A more organised route toward JLPT N5, N4, N3, N2 or N1
detailPage.tutorTitle: Matched with Japanese tutors for the right JLPT level.
detailPage.tutorDescription: Luna matches learners with tutors who can support the target JLPT level and explain Japanese clearly. Tutors help students balance exam preparation with practical language understanding so study feels structured and sustainable.

faq.q: Which JLPT levels can Luna support?
faq.a: We can support JLPT N5, N4, N3, N2 and N1 depending on the learner’s current level and tutor availability.

faq.q: Can beginners start with JLPT N5?
faq.a: Yes. Beginners can start with kana, basic vocabulary and grammar before moving into N5 mock practice.

faq.q: Is kanji included?
faq.a: Yes. Kanji and vocabulary are built according to the target level and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: Do lessons include listening?
faq.a: Yes. Listening practice is important at every JLPT level and becomes especially important from N3 upward.

seo.title: JLPT Preparation N5 to N1 | Japanese Vocabulary, Grammar & Listening | Luna Studies
seo.description: JLPT preparation for N5, N4, N3, N2 and N1 with Japanese vocabulary, kanji, grammar, reading, listening and mock question review.

hsk.title: HSK Preparation
hsk.body: Chinese proficiency preparation with level-based vocabulary growth, reading, listening and writing support.
hsk.details: HSK preparation is built around steady vocabulary growth and accurate use of Chinese sentence patterns. As students move through levels, they need more characters, stronger listening, faster reading and better control of grammar and written expression. Luna HSK Preparation helps learners build Mandarin proficiency in a structured way. Lessons combine vocabulary review, pinyin and character recognition where needed, sentence pattern practice, reading comprehension, listening tasks and writing support. We also help students connect exam preparation with real Mandarin communication so language growth feels useful beyond the test.
hsk.suitableFor: Students preparing for HSK exams, bilingual learners strengthening Chinese, and Mandarin learners who want a clear level-based study path.

detailPage.heroLine: Prepare for HSK with structured Mandarin vocabulary, grammar, listening, reading and writing practice.
detailPage.overviewTitle: HSK preparation built on vocabulary growth and usable Mandarin.
detailPage.overview: HSK success depends on building Chinese step by step: recognising characters, understanding sentence patterns, listening accurately and reading with increasing speed. Luna helps students organise study by level instead of jumping between random word lists. We review vocabulary in context, practise grammar through useful sentences and connect reading or listening tasks to real communication. For younger learners or heritage speakers, lessons can also support school Chinese while keeping future HSK pathways open.
detailPage.coverTitle: What we cover for HSK and Mandarin proficiency.
cover: HSK vocabulary growth by level, including character recognition, pinyin support and word usage
Chinese grammar patterns, sentence order, particles, measure words and common structures
Listening comprehension for dialogues, short passages, key details and natural phrasing
Reading practice for characters, sentences, short texts and exam-style questions
Writing support for sentence accuracy, paragraph building and school Chinese when needed
Review routines that recycle vocabulary so students remember and use new words
detailPage.approachTitle: Mandarin proficiency developed through level-based practice.
detailPage.approachDescription: We identify the learner’s current Mandarin level and target exam or school goal. Lessons then combine vocabulary, grammar, reading, listening and speaking reinforcement so students do not simply memorise word lists. Progress is reviewed through practical tasks and exam-style questions.
lunaHelps: Build HSK vocabulary gradually with context, review and active usage
Improve recognition of Chinese characters and sentence patterns
Strengthen listening comprehension for common HSK-style dialogues and prompts
Support reading fluency and written accuracy for Mandarin learners
Connect exam preparation with practical speaking and school Chinese goals
detailPage.outcomesTitle: HSK outcomes for Chinese language development.
outcomes: Larger Mandarin vocabulary and stronger character recognition
More accurate sentence patterns and grammar control
Improved listening comprehension for level-based tasks
Better reading fluency and confidence with Chinese texts
A clearer pathway through HSK levels and long-term Mandarin growth
detailPage.tutorTitle: Matched with Mandarin tutors for level-based Chinese learning.
detailPage.tutorDescription: Luna tutors help HSK learners study Chinese systematically. They adjust lessons for beginners, bilingual students or more advanced learners, making sure vocabulary, grammar, listening, reading and writing grow together.

faq.q: Which HSK levels can Luna support?
faq.a: We can support learners across different HSK levels, with lessons adjusted to current vocabulary, character knowledge and exam goals.

faq.q: Is HSK only vocabulary memorisation?
faq.a: No. Vocabulary matters, but students also need grammar, listening, reading accuracy and the ability to use words in context.

faq.q: Can heritage Chinese learners join?
faq.a: Yes. Lessons can support heritage learners who speak some Mandarin but need stronger reading and writing.

faq.q: Do lessons include speaking?
faq.a: Yes. Even when preparing for written HSK tasks, we include speaking practice when it helps students activate vocabulary.

seo.title: HSK Preparation | Mandarin Vocabulary, Reading & Listening | Luna Studies
seo.description: HSK preparation for Mandarin learners with level-based vocabulary growth, Chinese grammar, reading, listening, writing and practical communication support.

foundation.title: English Foundation
foundation.body: Long-term English development in grammar, vocabulary, reading comprehension and writing accuracy.
foundation.details: English Foundation is for students who need long-term language development rather than short exam cramming. Luna helps students build the core skills that support every future subject: grammar control, vocabulary depth, sentence writing, reading comprehension, paragraph structure and confidence using English in class. Lessons move patiently from diagnosis to targeted practice, so students know why a sentence works, how to understand a passage and how to express ideas more clearly. This program is especially useful for students transitioning into English-medium schools or catching up after gaps in learning.
foundation.suitableFor: Students who need stronger long-term English foundations, including grammar, vocabulary, reading, writing and classroom confidence.

detailPage.heroLine: Build a durable English foundation for school learning, reading confidence and clearer writing.
detailPage.overviewTitle: English foundations that support every future subject.
detailPage.overview: A weak English foundation affects far more than English class. Students may struggle to understand math word problems, science texts, humanities assignments or teacher feedback. Luna builds English from the ground up: grammar, vocabulary, sentence control, reading comprehension and paragraph writing. We do not rush students through worksheets. Tutors identify the exact gaps, explain language clearly and give practice that helps students use English with more independence. The goal is steady development that supports school success over the long term.
detailPage.coverTitle: What we cover for long-term English development.
cover: Grammar foundations: tense, agreement, clauses, punctuation, sentence types and common errors
Vocabulary growth through word families, context clues, academic words and active usage
Reading comprehension for fiction, nonfiction, main ideas, details, inference and evidence
Sentence writing, paragraph development, topic sentences and clear supporting details
School homework support when it reveals deeper English skill gaps
Confidence routines for students who avoid reading aloud, writing or answering in English
detailPage.approachTitle: Patient diagnosis, targeted practice and visible language growth.
detailPage.approachDescription: We start by finding the student’s missing foundations. Lessons then build skills in a logical order so students understand the language instead of memorising disconnected rules. Reading and writing are connected because stronger comprehension supports stronger expression.
lunaHelps: Identify grammar, vocabulary, reading and writing gaps that affect school performance
Explain English rules in a clear, student-friendly way
Build reading confidence through guided passages and discussion
Develop writing from sentences to paragraphs with regular feedback
Help students become more willing to use English in class and homework
detailPage.outcomesTitle: English foundation outcomes for school confidence.
outcomes: Stronger grammar accuracy and sentence control
Expanded vocabulary for school reading and writing
Better comprehension of fiction, nonfiction and academic passages
Clearer paragraph writing with organised ideas
More confidence participating in English-medium learning
detailPage.tutorTitle: Matched with patient tutors for long-term growth.
detailPage.tutorDescription: Foundation students need tutors who can explain simply without making lessons feel discouraging. Luna matches students with tutors who understand language gaps, build confidence and keep progress steady over time.

faq.q: Is English Foundation only for beginners?
faq.a: No. It also helps students who can communicate but still have grammar, reading or writing gaps.

faq.q: Can lessons support school homework?
faq.a: Yes. Homework can be used to identify deeper skill gaps, but lessons are not limited to homework completion.

faq.q: How long does foundation improvement take?
faq.a: Long-term English growth takes consistency. Many students improve gradually through regular reading, writing and feedback.

faq.q: Do you teach grammar directly?
faq.a: Yes. We teach grammar clearly and connect it to reading and writing tasks.

seo.title: English Foundation Tutoring | Grammar, Reading & Writing Support | Luna Studies
seo.description: Long-term English foundation tutoring for grammar, vocabulary, reading comprehension, sentence writing, paragraph development and school confidence.

speaking.title: Speaking & Writing
speaking.body: Communication, essay writing, presentation and academic expression skills for confident students.
speaking.details: Speaking & Writing is designed for students who need to express ideas clearly, not just answer worksheet questions. Luna helps learners build communication skills for discussions, presentations, interviews, essays and school assignments. Lessons develop idea generation, organisation, evidence, transitions, vocabulary choice, voice, fluency and confidence. Students learn how to plan before speaking or writing, structure their message for an audience and revise for clarity. This program is useful for international school students, bilingual learners and advanced students who want stronger academic expression.
speaking.suitableFor: Students who need better spoken communication, presentation confidence, essay writing, academic discussion or clearer expression in English.

detailPage.heroLine: Strengthen communication through speaking practice, essay writing and presentation coaching.
detailPage.overviewTitle: Speaking and writing support for students with ideas to express.
detailPage.overview: Many students understand content but struggle to explain it clearly. Luna’s Speaking & Writing program helps students turn thoughts into organised communication. In speaking lessons, students practise discussion, presentation, interview-style responses and fluency. In writing lessons, they learn how to plan essays, develop paragraphs, support arguments and revise sentences. The program builds confidence because students receive practical feedback on both message and delivery: what they are saying, how it is structured and how the audience receives it.
detailPage.coverTitle: What we cover in communication and academic writing.
cover: Presentation skills: structure, opening, transitions, voice, pacing, body language and confidence
Discussion skills: agreeing, disagreeing, explaining reasons, asking questions and developing ideas
Essay writing: thesis statements, paragraph structure, evidence, analysis and conclusions
Creative and reflective writing with stronger word choice and sentence variety
Interview-style speaking and school oral assessment preparation
Revision habits for clarity, coherence, grammar and audience impact
detailPage.approachTitle: Ideas first, structure next, confidence through practice.
detailPage.approachDescription: We help students plan what they want to say before focusing on polished language. Tutors model strong responses, guide practice and give feedback on organisation, clarity, vocabulary and delivery. Students gradually become more independent communicators.
lunaHelps: Teach students how to organise spoken and written ideas logically
Build fluency and confidence through repeated speaking practice
Improve essay structure, paragraph development and argument clarity
Support presentations, interviews, oral assessments and class discussions
Give revision feedback that makes writing clearer and more convincing
detailPage.outcomesTitle: Communication outcomes for school and beyond.
outcomes: More confident presentations and spoken responses
Clearer essay structure and paragraph organisation
Stronger vocabulary choice for academic and creative expression
Better ability to explain, persuade and support ideas
Improved confidence in discussions, interviews and school assignments
detailPage.tutorTitle: Matched with tutors who coach expression and confidence.
detailPage.tutorDescription: Speaking and writing growth requires supportive feedback. Luna tutors help students organise ideas, practise delivery and revise writing so communication becomes clearer, more natural and more purposeful.

faq.q: Is this program only for weak English students?
faq.a: No. It is also valuable for strong students who want better presentation, essay and academic communication skills.

faq.q: Can Luna help with school presentations?
faq.a: Yes. We can help students plan, script, rehearse and improve delivery for presentations.

faq.q: Do you teach essay writing?
faq.a: Yes. Lessons can focus on thesis statements, paragraph structure, evidence, analysis and revision.

faq.q: Can shy students join?
faq.a: Yes. We build speaking confidence gradually with guided practice and supportive feedback.

seo.title: Speaking and Writing Tutoring | Essays, Presentations & Communication | Luna Studies
seo.description: Speaking and writing support for communication confidence, essay structure, presentations, discussion skills and academic expression.

math.title: Math Support
math.body: Curriculum math support focused on concepts, problem-solving and confidence with school assessments.
math.details: Math Support at Luna is designed around understanding, not just answer getting. Students often struggle because they missed a concept earlier, rush calculations, misread word problems or cannot explain their reasoning. Luna tutors identify these gaps and rebuild skills through clear instruction, guided examples and targeted practice. Lessons can support international school math, Singapore-style topics, MAP Math, AEIS Math or general grade-level curriculum needs. We focus on number sense, algebraic thinking, geometry, measurement, data, problem-solving strategies and exam confidence.
math.suitableFor: Students who need curriculum math support, stronger problem-solving skills, help with word problems or more confidence in school assessments.

detailPage.heroLine: Strengthen mathematical understanding, calculation accuracy and problem-solving confidence.
detailPage.overviewTitle: Math support that explains the why behind the answer.
detailPage.overview: A student may get a math answer wrong for many reasons: weak concept knowledge, careless arithmetic, confusing wording, poor diagram use or lack of strategy. Luna Math Support separates these issues so lessons can target the real problem. Tutors explain concepts clearly, model problem-solving steps and help students practise until methods become reliable. Whether the student needs school homework support, exam review or deeper reasoning development, the goal is to build confidence through understanding rather than short-term memorisation.
detailPage.coverTitle: What we cover in curriculum math and problem solving.
cover: Number sense, operations, fractions, decimals, percentages, ratios and proportional reasoning
Algebraic thinking, equations, patterns, functions and symbolic reasoning by grade level
Geometry, measurement, angles, area, volume, coordinate geometry and visual reasoning
Data handling, graphs, probability, statistics and interpretation questions
Word-problem strategies, model drawing, multi-step reasoning and answer checking
School test review, homework clarification and targeted practice for weak topics
detailPage.approachTitle: Concept clarity before speed and exam pressure.
detailPage.approachDescription: We first identify whether the student needs concept reteaching, procedural fluency, problem-solving strategy or confidence. Lessons then move from explanation to guided practice to independent attempts, with mistake review built into the process.
lunaHelps: Find the concept gaps behind repeated math mistakes
Explain methods clearly using examples, diagrams and step-by-step reasoning
Teach students how to decode word problems and choose operations
Build accuracy through targeted practice and checking routines
Support school curriculum while strengthening long-term problem-solving habits
detailPage.outcomesTitle: Math outcomes students can carry into class and exams.
outcomes: Stronger conceptual understanding across key math topics
Better calculation accuracy and fewer careless mistakes
Improved word-problem reasoning and strategy selection
More confidence explaining mathematical thinking
More consistent performance in school assessments and homework
detailPage.tutorTitle: Matched with tutors who make math understandable.
detailPage.tutorDescription: Luna matches math students with tutors who can explain concepts patiently and adapt to the student’s curriculum. Tutors help students rebuild weak foundations, practise strategically and approach problems with more confidence.

faq.q: Which math curricula can Luna support?
faq.a: We can support international school math, Singapore-style topics, MAP Math, AEIS Math and general grade-level mathematics depending on the tutor match.

faq.q: Can lessons focus on word problems?
faq.a: Yes. We teach students how to read, model, plan and solve multi-step word problems.

faq.q: Is homework help included?
faq.a: Homework can be included, but we also use it to identify and fix deeper concept gaps.

faq.q: Can Luna help students who fear math?
faq.a: Yes. Clear explanations, small wins and structured practice can help rebuild confidence.

seo.title: Math Tutoring and Problem-Solving Support | Luna Studies
seo.description: Personalised math support for curriculum learning, word problems, conceptual understanding, calculation accuracy and exam confidence.

japanese.title: Japanese Lessons
japanese.body: Practical Japanese communication lessons for conversation, grammar, reading, writing and school support.
japanese.details: Japanese Lessons at Luna focus on practical communication as well as structured language foundations. Students may need Japanese for daily life, school, travel, family communication or future JLPT study. Lessons can include hiragana, katakana, kanji, grammar patterns, vocabulary, conversation, reading and writing. We help learners move from memorising phrases to understanding how Japanese sentences work. Tutors adjust lessons for complete beginners, international school students, bilingual learners and students living in Japan who want to participate more confidently in everyday Japanese situations.
japanese.suitableFor: Learners who want practical Japanese communication, school Japanese support, beginner foundations, conversation confidence or a pathway toward JLPT later.

detailPage.heroLine: Learn Japanese for real communication, school support and long-term language confidence.
detailPage.overviewTitle: Japanese lessons that connect grammar with real communication.
detailPage.overview: Learning Japanese can feel overwhelming because students must manage new scripts, sentence order, particles, grammar levels and cultural context. Luna makes Japanese more approachable by connecting structure with practical use. Students learn vocabulary and grammar through meaningful sentences, conversation practice and reading or writing tasks. Lessons can be gentle for beginners or more advanced for students who already know some Japanese. The aim is not only to pass exercises, but to help students understand and use Japanese more naturally.
detailPage.coverTitle: What we cover in Japanese language lessons.
cover: Hiragana, katakana, kanji foundations and reading confidence for beginners
Japanese grammar patterns, particles, verb forms, sentence endings and natural usage
Conversation practice for introductions, daily routines, school life and real situations
Listening and pronunciation support for more natural communication
Reading and writing practice for school tasks, short passages and personal expression
Optional JLPT pathway planning when students are ready for exam preparation
detailPage.approachTitle: Structured Japanese foundations with practical speaking built in.
detailPage.approachDescription: We balance accuracy and communication. Students learn how Japanese works, then practise using it in sentences, dialogues and short tasks. Tutors adapt the pace based on age, background, school needs and confidence level.
lunaHelps: Build kana, kanji, vocabulary and grammar in a logical order
Help students understand particles and sentence patterns clearly
Develop speaking confidence for daily life and classroom situations
Support school Japanese assignments, reading and writing when needed
Prepare a smooth transition into JLPT study if the student later chooses that path
detailPage.outcomesTitle: Japanese outcomes for practical language confidence.
outcomes: Stronger understanding of Japanese sentence structure and grammar
Expanded vocabulary for daily life, school and personal topics
Improved confidence in basic or intermediate conversation
Better reading and writing foundations with kana and kanji
A clearer long-term path for Japanese study or JLPT preparation
detailPage.tutorTitle: Matched with Japanese tutors for the learner’s real goal.
detailPage.tutorDescription: Luna Japanese tutors adapt lessons for communication, school support, beginner foundations or future JLPT preparation. Students receive clear explanations and practical speaking opportunities so Japanese becomes usable, not just studied.

faq.q: Can complete beginners join Japanese lessons?
faq.a: Yes. Beginners can start with kana, basic vocabulary, pronunciation and simple sentence patterns.

faq.q: Are lessons only for JLPT preparation?
faq.a: No. Students can learn Japanese for daily communication, school, travel, family or personal interest.

faq.q: Can Luna support school Japanese?
faq.a: Yes. Lessons can include homework, reading, writing and curriculum support.

faq.q: Will students practise speaking?
faq.a: Yes. Practical conversation is included so students can use Japanese with more confidence.

seo.title: Japanese Lessons for Practical Communication | Luna Studies
seo.description: Personalised Japanese lessons for beginners and students covering conversation, grammar, kana, kanji, reading, writing and school support.

mandarin.title: Mandarin Lessons
mandarin.body: Mandarin speaking, reading and writing lessons for communication, school Chinese and bilingual growth.
mandarin.details: Mandarin Lessons at Luna help students build Chinese as a living language: speaking clearly, understanding what they hear, reading characters and writing with accuracy. Some learners need school Chinese support, some are heritage speakers who understand Mandarin but struggle with literacy, and others are complete beginners. Lessons can include pronunciation, tones, pinyin, character recognition, vocabulary, sentence patterns, reading comprehension and composition. We connect speaking, reading and writing so students can use Mandarin more confidently in school, family or future HSK pathways.
mandarin.suitableFor: Students learning Mandarin for communication, school Chinese, bilingual development, heritage language growth, reading and writing improvement or future HSK study.

detailPage.heroLine: Build Mandarin confidence through speaking, reading, writing and structured Chinese language practice.
detailPage.overviewTitle: Mandarin lessons that connect conversation with literacy.
detailPage.overview: Mandarin learners often have uneven skills: some can speak but cannot read characters, while others can complete worksheets but hesitate in conversation. Luna builds Mandarin in a balanced way. Students practise pronunciation and tones, learn useful vocabulary, read characters in context and develop sentence or composition writing step by step. Lessons can support international school Chinese, bilingual family goals or beginner Mandarin learning. The aim is to help students understand Chinese, use it actively and grow toward stronger literacy over time.
detailPage.coverTitle: What we cover in Mandarin language lessons.
cover: Speaking practice: pronunciation, tones, daily conversation, storytelling and question responses
Pinyin, character recognition, stroke awareness and vocabulary growth by theme
Reading comprehension for sentences, short passages, school texts and graded materials
Writing support: sentence building, paragraph writing, composition planning and accuracy
Chinese grammar patterns, measure words, connectors and natural sentence order
School Chinese support or optional HSK pathway planning when appropriate
detailPage.approachTitle: Balanced Mandarin growth across speaking, reading and writing.
detailPage.approachDescription: We adapt lessons to the learner’s background: beginner, bilingual, heritage speaker or school Chinese student. Tutors connect oral practice with character learning and writing tasks so Mandarin skills reinforce each other instead of developing separately.
lunaHelps: Strengthen pronunciation, tones and speaking confidence through guided conversation
Build character recognition and vocabulary with regular review
Improve reading comprehension for Chinese texts and school assignments
Develop sentence writing and composition skills step by step
Support bilingual learners, heritage speakers and beginners with different needs
detailPage.outcomesTitle: Mandarin outcomes for communication and literacy.
outcomes: More confident Mandarin speaking and clearer pronunciation
Expanded Chinese vocabulary and stronger character recognition
Better reading fluency and comprehension of Chinese texts
Improved sentence writing, paragraph structure and composition confidence
A stronger foundation for school Chinese, bilingual growth or future HSK study
detailPage.tutorTitle: Matched with Mandarin tutors for the student’s language background.
detailPage.tutorDescription: Luna matches Mandarin learners with tutors who understand their starting point, whether they are beginners, bilingual students or heritage speakers. Lessons balance encouragement, structure and practical use so Chinese becomes more active and confident.

faq.q: Can beginners learn Mandarin with Luna?
faq.a: Yes. Beginners can start with tones, pinyin, basic vocabulary, simple characters and everyday conversation.

faq.q: Can heritage speakers improve reading and writing?
faq.a: Yes. We can help students who speak Mandarin at home but need stronger character recognition, reading and composition.

faq.q: Do lessons support school Chinese?
faq.a: Yes. Tutors can support school vocabulary, reading, writing, homework and assessment preparation.

faq.q: Is HSK included?
faq.a: HSK preparation can be added when the student has a clear exam goal or wants a level-based Mandarin pathway.

seo.title: Mandarin Lessons for Speaking, Reading and Writing | Luna Studies
seo.description: Mandarin lessons for speaking confidence, tones, pinyin, Chinese reading, writing, school support, bilingual learners and future HSK pathways.

subjectDetail.coverTitle: Lessons focused on what your child actually needs.
subjectDetail.approachDescription: We do not just assign worksheets. Luna combines diagnosis, planning, personalised tutoring, homework feedback and progress tracking.

subjectDetail.tutorTitle: Matched with the right Luna tutor.

seo.title: Why Families Choose LUNA Studies | Personalised International Education
seo.description: Discover how LUNA Studies (Luna Education) supports students through personalised learning plans, tutor matching, academic tracking and international school preparation.

hero.label: Our Approach
hero.description: Luna Education combines elite tutors, personalised planning, structured assessments, and continuous support — so students don’t just attend lessons, they improve with direction.

highlights.questionBanks: Free question banks for registered students

typical.label: Typical tuition
typical.point1: Random worksheets without a clear learning path
typical.point3: Parents unsure whether progress is real

luna.label: Luna Education
luna.point3: Pre-test, mid-test, and post-test quality checks
luna.point4: Tutors matched by background, subject, and student needs

values.label: Our Values
values.description: At Luna Education, we don’t believe in random practice or passive learning. We focus on clarity, structure, and measurable improvement.

clarity.title: Clarity
clarity.text: Students should understand why an answer is correct, not just memorise it.

structure.title: Structure
structure.text: Learning should follow a clear path with direction and purpose.

growth.title: Growth
growth.text: Mistakes are not failures. They are signals for improvement.

responsibility.title: Responsibility
responsibility.text: Students take ownership while tutors guide and challenge them.

bullets.point1: Lessons follow a clear structure instead of random topics
bullets.point2: Mistakes are tracked and reviewed systematically
bullets.point3: Students learn how to explain answers, not just give them

tutors.title: Elite tutors with international backgrounds
tutors.text: Our tutors are young, driven, and carefully selected from strong academic backgrounds. We match students with tutors who understand different school systems, countries, and exam expectations.

planning.title: Personal planning for every student
planning.text: Every student receives a customised plan based on their level, target school, exam, and weaknesses. Lessons are not random — each step is designed with a purpose.

progress.title: Progress you can actually see
progress.text: We use pre-tests, mid-tests, and post-tests to check quality and improvement. Parents can clearly understand where the student started and how they are growing.

support.title: Support beyond lesson time
support.text: Registered students receive access to question banks and chat support for learning queries, so learning continues even outside class.

questionBank.label: Registered Student Benefit
questionBank.description: Registered students can practise with structured question banks matched to their goals. Tutors can then review mistakes and turn practice into targeted improvement.

review.label: Tutor Review
review.text: Mistakes become the next lesson plan.

cta.label: Start your journey ✨
cta.description: We’ll assess the student’s current level, identify strengths and weaknesses, and recommend a personalised learning plan designed around their goals.

items.bio: Specialises in building strong English foundations through patient guidance and structured language learning.
experience: English foundation building for younger learners
Grammar, reading, and writing support
Structured language-learning guidance
Patient coaching for confidence and communication skills
items.quote: Christine helps students grow English foundations step by step with patient guidance and steady encouragement.

items.name: Kana
items.bio: A patient and structured Japanese tutor who helps students build strong foundations in grammar, vocabulary, speaking, and reading. She supports learners in gaining confidence through clear explanations and practical communication practice.
experience: Beginner to intermediate Japanese language tutoring
JLPT vocabulary and grammar preparation
Conversation practice for daily and academic situations
Support for students adapting to Japanese school or daily life
items.quote: Kana helps learners use Japanese in real situations through clear grammar, practical speaking and supportive practice.

items.name: Ruth
items.bio: Ruth supports young learners and bilingual students in building natural English fluency, clearer pronunciation, reading confidence and everyday communication skills through warm, interactive lessons.
experience: English language teaching for young learners and bilingual students
Speaking fluency, pronunciation, and classroom communication coaching
Reading comprehension, vocabulary, and phonics support
Writing foundations, sentence structure, and grammar guidance
Interactive confidence-building lessons for everyday English use
items.quote: Ruth helps students speak English more naturally through friendly interaction, clear correction and confidence-building practice.

items.name: Siya
items.bio: Siya guides students in independent thinking, research habits, academic writing and global-perspective discussions, helping learners organise ideas clearly and express arguments with confidence.
experience: Academic research and source evaluation
Essay writing structure and argument development
Critical thinking and evidence-based discussion
Global Perspectives project support
Independent research planning and study habits
Academic presentation preparation and delivery
items.quote: Siya helps students ask stronger questions, research independently and express global ideas with clarity.

sections.title: 1. Information We Collect
sections.text: We may collect information such as names, email addresses, lesson preferences, student levels, and communication history when you use our services or submit enquiries.

sections.title: 2. How We Use Information
sections.text: Your information is used to provide tutoring services, manage accounts, respond to enquiries, improve learning support, and communicate important updates.

sections.title: 3. Student Data Protection
sections.text: We take reasonable steps to protect student information and restrict access to authorised tutors, staff, and administrators only.

sections.title: 4. Payments and Third-Party Services
sections.text: Some services may involve trusted third-party providers such as payment processors, email systems, scheduling tools, or analytics platforms.

sections.title: 5. Cookies and Analytics
sections.text: Our website may use cookies or analytics tools to improve user experience, understand website usage, and maintain platform functionality.

sections.title: 6. Sharing of Information
sections.text: We do not sell personal information. Information may only be shared where necessary for educational support, legal compliance, or operational purposes.

sections.title: 7. Data Retention
sections.text: We may retain account and learning information for operational, educational, and administrative purposes unless deletion is requested.

sections.title: 8. Contact
sections.text: For privacy-related questions, please contact us at enquiries@lunastudies.com.

sections.title: 1. Use of Our Services
sections.text: LUNA Studies provides tutoring, academic support, consultation, and related educational services. By using our website or services, you agree to use them responsibly and lawfully.

sections.title: 2. Student Accounts
sections.text: Students, tutors, and administrators may access protected areas using approved accounts or invite codes. You are responsible for keeping your login details secure.

sections.title: 3. Lessons and Scheduling
sections.text: Lesson schedules, formats, and tutor arrangements may vary depending on availability, student needs, and agreed learning plans.

sections.title: 4. Payments and Cancellations
sections.text: Fees, payment timing, cancellation rules, and rescheduling policies will be communicated separately before or during enrolment.

sections.title: 5. Learning Materials
sections.text: Worksheets, question banks, lesson materials, and feedback documents are provided for personal learning use only and may not be copied, shared, sold, or redistributed without permission.

sections.title: 6. Limitation of Guarantee
sections.text: We aim to support student improvement through structured tutoring, but we cannot guarantee specific exam scores, school admission results, or academic outcomes.

sections.title: 7. Changes to Terms
sections.text: We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use of our services means you accept the updated terms.

sections.title: 8. Contact
sections.text: For questions about these terms, please contact us at enquiries@lunastudies.com.

english-tutor.title: English Tutor
english-tutor.about: Luna Education is looking for reliable and patient English tutors to support international school students through personalised lessons and structured learning guidance.
responsibilities: Deliver 1-to-1 English lessons for international school students.
Support students in reading, writing, speaking, school-based English, and exam preparation.
Prepare lessons based on each student’s level, goals, and learning needs.
Provide short lesson feedback after each class.
Follow Luna’s learning plans and support students’ long-term progress.
requirements: Strong English communication skills.
Teaching or tutoring experience preferred.
Patient, responsible, and clear explanation style.
Comfortable working with children or teenagers.
International school or exam preparation experience is a plus.
workingArrangement: In-person teaching may be available in Tokyo.

math-tutor.title: Mathematics Tutor
math-tutor.about: Luna Education is looking for mathematics tutors who can help students strengthen concepts, problem-solving skills, and school-based learning.
responsibilities: Deliver 1-to-1 mathematics lessons.
Support students with school math, problem solving, MAP Math, AEIS Math, and curriculum-based learning.
Prepare lessons according to each student’s level and weak areas.
Explain mistakes clearly and help students build confidence.
Provide short lesson feedback after each class.
requirements: Strong mathematics foundation.
Teaching or tutoring experience preferred.
Clear and logical explanation style.
Comfortable working with children or teenagers.
International school, AEIS, MAP, IGCSE, or similar experience is a plus.
workingArrangement: In-person teaching may be available in Tokyo.

chinese-tutor.title: Chinese Tutor
chinese-tutor.about: Luna Education is looking for Chinese tutors who can support international school and bilingual students in Mandarin learning.
responsibilities: Deliver 1-to-1 Chinese lessons.
Support students with speaking, reading, writing, school Chinese, HSK, and bilingual learning.
Adjust lessons based on each student’s language background.
Provide short lesson feedback after each class.
Help students build confidence using Mandarin.
requirements: Strong Mandarin communication skills.
Teaching or tutoring experience preferred.
Patient and responsible teaching style.
Comfortable working with children or teenagers.
International school or bilingual teaching experience is a plus.

academic-coordinator.title: Academic Coordinator
academic-coordinator.about: Luna Education is looking for an Academic Coordinator to support families, schools, tutors, and students through structured learning follow-up and academic service coordination.
responsibilities: Serve as the main point of contact between families, schools, and tutors.
Support student learning planning and academic progress follow-up.
Coordinate communication with schools, parents, and tutors.
Manage lesson feedback, student records, and learning reports.
Assist with tutor matching and educational planning.
Support Luna’s student success and academic support services.
requirements: Strong communication and organisation skills.
Responsible, detail-oriented, and professional.
Comfortable communicating with parents, schools, and tutors.
Education, tutoring, or student support background preferred.
Able to work independently and follow up consistently.
workingArrangement: Online work with possible on-site support.
School and parent communication may be required.

seo.title: Luna Arcade Learning Games | Vocabulary Practice Demo | LUNA Studies
seo.description: Try Luna Arcade, a public demo of LUNA Studies learning games for vocabulary practice, retention, bilingual learners and international school students.

hero.label: Luna Arcade
hero.title: Experience Learning With Luna
hero.subtitle: Explore our vocabulary games, learning system and personalised educational approach.
hero.visualAlt: Hapiko presenting Luna Arcade vocabulary games

why.title: Learning Should Feel Engaging
why.text: Luna Arcade turns repeated practice into short, focused challenges that support vocabulary retention, confidence, consistency and motivation.

demo.title: Try Luna Arcade
demo.subtitle: Play our free demo below. No registration required.
demo.wordInstruction: Demo mode: tap each listed word after finding it in the grid.

notice.title: 🎮 Demo Version
notice.description: The public demo is designed for quick exploration before creating an account.
items: Demo vocabulary pool is limited to approximately 50 words/pairs

demo.error: The public demo vocabulary is not ready yet. Please try again later or book a consultation to learn more.

ecosystem.title: More Than Just Games
ecosystem.text: Luna Arcade is part of a wider learning ecosystem that connects diagnosis, lessons, practice, review and parent visibility.

items.title: MAP / WIDA learners
items.description: Build steady English vocabulary for adaptive MAP Growth tasks and WIDA language domains.

items.title: CAT4 reasoning practice
items.description: Use visual memory and pattern-style practice to support verbal, non-verbal and spatial thinking.

items.title: AEIS preparation
items.description: Strengthen core English vocabulary and confidence for Singapore school entry preparation.

items.title: International school students
items.description: Keep academic English practice active between lessons, school assignments and assessments.

items.title: Bilingual learners
items.description: Connect meanings across languages through repeated recall, image support and playful review.

items.title: English vocabulary learners
items.description: Practise high-frequency words through short challenges that make review easier to repeat.

items.a: They are most useful when connected to a learning plan. Luna Arcade reinforces vocabulary and confidence between lessons.

items.q: How does Luna Arcade support vocabulary learning?
items.a: Students meet words repeatedly through matching, searching, recall and review activities.

items.q: Is Luna Arcade suitable for international school students?
items.a: Yes. It supports academic vocabulary, bilingual learners and students preparing for international school expectations.
In the full Luna ecosystem, progress monitoring helps parents see consistency, practice and learning growth.

items.q: What ages is Luna Arcade designed for?
items.a: The current learning flow is designed for primary and middle-school learners, with Grade 1-6 progression.

cta.title: Ready To Start Learning With Luna?
cta.description: Book a consultation and receive a personalised learning recommendation.
